MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-276766 WITH CASE CGC-17-276629
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC.
Emily Anselmo of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant General Electric Company and Viacom CBS Inc.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion to Consolidate Two Wrongful Death/Brayton/Preference Cases, or In the Alternative, Motion for Joinder of Cases Pursuant to C.C.P. 378, and to Proceed with Trial by Alternative Means is DENIED without prejudice. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs fail to sufficiently show a common question of law or fact as required for consolidation and joinder. (C.C.P. 1048(a) and 378(a)(1).) Plaintiffs fail to satisfy the Malcolm factors of common worksite, similar occupation, similar time of exposure, and type of disease. (Malcolm v. Nat'l Gypsum Co. (1993) 995 F.2d 346, 350-51.) Regarding Plaintiffs' request for the Court to order the trials to proceed "by alternative means" and that the unified trials proceed on the earliest dates, the trial judge has discretion to determine the manner and procedures to be used at trial, as consistent with health and safety directives of the state and county authority. Each case will proceed on the trial dates set in the case sought to be consolidated, as was ordered by the Court when preference in that case was granted. Court to issue order and serve order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-589-3651) csrkim@comcast.net (503/CML)
